_comment: CONTEXT_PACKET_CONTRACT (ABS-P4 / AR-2.2, 260713). The versioned schema of the customer-safe, auditable record of the context an agent was given for one governed action: intent -> resolved capability (role/skills/tools) -> the role-scoped context scope (counts) -> redaction -> a deterministic fingerprint. Built by src/workers/lib/context-packet.ts (pure kernel). Persistence (context_packets table) + the assembler wire are the next operator-gated steps. CUSTOMER-SAFE INVARIANT: this record NEVER carries internal ids, event/document ids, graph topology, prompts, or skill bodies - only counts, coarse capability labels, and a content fingerprint.

fields.tenant: workspace id (internal-linkage field, not surfaced to other tenants)
fields.intent: the intent ref this context served, or null
fields.selected_skills: [{key, version}] the skills the resolver selected (customer_visible catalog keys)
fields.allowed_tools: string[] tools granted for this action
fields.denied_tools: string[] tools denied (internal tools stay named-but-denied, never invoked)
fields.context_scope: {event_count, document_count, unpromoted_document_count, source_count} - COUNTS ONLY, never ids
fields.redaction: {profile, client_empty} - which redaction profile the assembler applied
fields.context_fingerprint: deterministic FNV-1a content id (dedup + change-detection; excludes timestamp + receipt_ref)
fields.receipt_ref: the resolution receipt id this packet links to, or null

invariants: context_fingerprint is stable for the same (tenant, role, mode, intent, skills, tools, scope-counts, redaction) - independent of generated_at and receipt_ref
no field in forbidden_fields ever appears in a built packet
client_empty=true implies an empty grounding bundle (context_scope counts may still describe what was excluded)
